item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last note STILL has me laughing. Instant +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so sorry to say this, but this image may not be compliant with SCP Wiki license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

According to Wikimedia Commons, Unsplash switched its sitewide license from CC0 to its own license on 2017/06/05. This custom license has prohibited to "compile photos from Unsplash to replicate a similar or competing service", so I think it does not meet Creative Commons license requirements.

I'm currently not sure whether old Unsplash images prior 2017/06/05 are still considered as CC0 or not. However, this image was uploaded on 2019/06/11 and thusly is not under any kind of Creative Commons.

Edit: After making contact Elogee FishTruck, I was notified that the Unsplash License is considered to be okay so far! Thanks, and I'm sorry for troubling you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://unsplash.com/photos/w8Wt2mDqKJ8

Some images are worth a thousand words. This image is worth at least 9.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did not liked the explanation for getting rid of cameras in the cargo. I believe the truck was made to easily steal valuables, therefore any recording in the cargo could be considered evidence.
I personally would assume the "getting rid of recorders" as a self defense mechanism.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time. Per author request, only the link to the Blueprint page was made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got two potential links here. First off, the mention of anomalous larceny immediately reminds me of the newest GoI on the block The Chicago Spirit. I don't feel its too far out of the realm of possibility for the SCPF to research whether this was something done by the group or one of its splinters. Additionally, the containment procedures could be tweaked from a simple secure parking lot to a Anomalous Vehicle Containment Bay, which has been used in a fair number of articles and gives that "Standardized containment" feel. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1492 is currently impounded in the Site-47 secure parking lot with tires and battery removed. Personnel are reminded not to bring precious minerals or stones within fifty meters of the vehicle at any time.

To this:

SCP-1492 is currently impounded in the Site-47 Anomalous Vehicle Containment Bay with tires and battery removed. Personnel are reminded not to bring precious minerals or stones within fifty meters of the vehicle at any time.

Changing this:

SCP-1492 is a civilian-model armored car which has been modified for use in anomalous larceny. The passenger's seat has been replaced with an experimental target acquisition and teleportation device that Foundation technicians have thus far failed to replicate. When activated, the device identifies nearby objects of value out to a range of roughly forty meters and transports them into the rear cargo compartment regardless of intervening materials or subject mass.

To this:

SCP-1492 is a civilian-model armored car which has been modified for use in anomalous larceny.1 The passenger's seat has been replaced with an experimental target acquisition and teleportation device that Foundation technicians have thus far failed to replicate. When activated, the device identifies nearby objects of value out to a range of roughly forty meters and transports them into the rear cargo compartment regardless of intervening materials or subject mass.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Various Groups of Interest, including remnants of the Chicago Spirit are being investigated as potential responsible parties for SCP-1492's modifications.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One nitpick.

We won't test people in it for safety concerns?

really?

really.

That's what D-Class are FOR.

Still +1 because of the notes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a gold case

That's what I was thinking!!!

item.title: A diamond among the 1400-series
item.content:encoded:
This is great. One of the few skips where I want to see the attached log expanded rather than deleted.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just okay, but I quite like the IOUs. +1'ed

item.title: Re: MAKE the camera more valuable then
item.content:encoded: you're assuming that the cameras get teleported out to make more room for loot. Maybe they get teleported out because the effect only works when there are no witnesses.

item.title: MAKE the camera more valuable then
item.content:encoded: I don't see why it didn't occur to the Foundation Staff to contract someone to make an artistically crafted video camera. They could gold-plate it, then embed jewels on it and make the digital sensors on the inside of the utmost precious materials, couldn't they?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it, so few SCPs have practical applications. You'd think that in a universe where crazy-ass shit like this can be found literally lying on the ground, someone would have used it for crime before now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I guess it's good to know that, despite having been gone all weekend, I don't actually have to read any of the new SCPs before upvoting them. Catching up will be a breeze!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My god, those IOUs (except for the last one) read like a list of those shitty "365 romantic things to say/do to/for your lover" books/calendars that are NEVER ROMANTIC.
Also, I still love this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone should check the offices of the senior staff, one of them probably has one. With "Pimp" embossed on the side in platinum.

Just remember to clear off any tapes/data already on there. Without looking at it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have we tried a gold plated camera with diamond lenses to view it during teleports?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is noted that it seems to consider subjective value? Maybe it reflects the thoughts of whoever made it… Or was used in its construction… And they had a bad experience trying to fence stolen cameras? Or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure current standards also say that SCPs should not rely heavily on "throw more D-class at it". Even if you subscribe to the "evil science bastards" outlook, D-class are still a Resource and should not be used up trivially, especially when they're likely to just say "it appeared suddenly".

It would be more worthwhile on a number of levels to just make a valuable camera. One with sentimental attachment or a gold case or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My foundation, headcanon-wise, doesn't solve problems by throwing d-class at things, and takes the lives of its personnel seriously. There's no reason to risk somebody getting a wedding ring stuck in their brain, since knowing how the device works is not important to containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was extremely boring and I was ready to downvote until I read the last IOU. I don't know how you did it, but that completely reversed my vote. Thank you for brightening my day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely loved this - especially the IOU for the wedding ring :) Bit conflicted on the photographic equipment bit, though - professional photography and cinematographic equipment can be monstrously expensive and would definitely have extreme value to its owner (recently read about Canon's http://technorati.com/technology/gadgets/article/canon-unveils-one-of-their-most/ latest effort], expected to come with a $15,000 price tag). An expensive camera could be set to take photos at regular intervals then introduced into 1492's larceny field.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sentimental value when only gold, currency and similar items are processed by the SCP? Maybe I interprreted it wrongly, but sentimental items can be other things, like photographs or so. Or maybe it was meant to sound "sentimental items made of rich metals, etc." ?

Experiments with human observers in the cargo area have been postponed due to the dangers posed by objects spontaneously materializing in a confined space.

Why don't I really buy this from an organisation often solving its' problems by "Throw more D-class" at it? Oh well.

It was an ok SCP. The note at the end was nice (I wanna know what was that last SCP…) No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To me, is the very definition of when people talk about how "just weird" can work. Super beuno.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been said already, but the last note really is the strawberry on the icing on the cake. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the last note alone. Beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at 'anomalous larceny.'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, nice. I remember this, of course. I liked it then, and I like it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author self deletion a week or two ago. It's now been reposted.

I hadn't seen this one before. Glad I got to see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Déjà vu? What happened to the old article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love that last IOU. :)
